summary: High‑energy 8/12/81 Salt Lake show with scorching Scarlet > Fire and a legendary Althea, captured on mostly solid soundboard tape despite occasional balance quirks.
review: The performance is widely hailed as one of the era’s best – Jerry’s incendiary Fire runs, the soaring Scarlet > Fire sequence, and an unforgettable Althea dominate the second set, while the first set is seen as merely serviceable. Recording quality varies: most listeners praise the later A+ soundboard sections, though a few note early audience patches, muddy spots, or balance issues.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating is ~4.7; the show receives overwhelming praise while recording quality is generally good with some mixed comments, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Primarily soundboard (SBD) with occasional audience patches; later sections described as A+ SBD, but early parts can be muddy or “cardboard,” and balance issues (Bob forward, Jerry recessed) are reported by a few reviewers.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is highlighted as a seamless, high‑octane journey with tight band interplay; first set is noted as weaker but still enjoyable.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for out‑of‑this‑world Fire runs and inspired playing throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned positively in a few reviews for locking in with Jerry and Bob during the Althea climax.
band_member_comments.Bob: Balance issues noted by one reviewer (too forward), otherwise not singled out negatively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ receives strong commendation for presence and tone, though some note it can dominate the mix on other releases.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s “Jack A. Roe” solo is highlighted as a standout moment; overall drumming described as tight.
